title: Any way to trigger an automation relative to the time in a date column?
excerpt: Hi all, I’m wondering if there is any way to trigger an automation relative to the time in a date column?  All of the date based automation triggers that I have seen ask you to pre-select one time for every occurrence of…

posts.author: botsquad (Patrick Fallon)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all, I’m wondering if there is any way to trigger an automation relative to the time in a date column? All of the date based automation triggers that I have seen ask you to pre-select one time for every occurrence of the Automation. But I am trying to trigger an automation 1.5 hours before the time in a particualr date column.
Seems like it’s not possible but hopefully somebody can prove me wrong?
Cheers, Patrick

posts.author: Kofi (Kofi FiftyTechSolutions.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Patrick,
It is certainly possible, I have made automations to send email reminders 1 hour before it is due. You need a tool like Make.com to do it. Are you able to give more detail on what automation you are trigger with the automation? I can certainly help!

Thanks,

Kofi
FiftyTechSolutions.com

posts.author: workflowboost (Bartosz @ Workflow Boost)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey! While this isn’t a native feature in monday yet, you can use the Advanced Time Triggers app to set up actions based on dates and times saved in a date column.

image1458×593 106 KB

The triggers are available in the automation builder, allowing you to create custom automations to trigger any action you need.
